summary: Bundled Groq API key silently relays user AI prompts through the author's account
details: The wheel ships `epl/.ai_config.json` containing a hardcoded Groq provider secret (`gsk_KIFZYR...P0Ps9Sq`). On module load, `epl/ai.py:_load_config()` reads this file and assigns `CLOUD_API_KEY = cfg.get('api_key')`. Any caller of the package's documented `ai.generate` / `ai.chat` / `ai.code_assist` / `ai.explain_error` APIs hits the cloud path by default (both provider and key are present), and the prompt content is POSTed to `https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions` with `Authorization: Bearer {CLOUD_API_KEY}` — the author's bundled key. Effect on users: prompts (which typically contain the user's source code, error traces, or other text being debugged) are routed through the author's Groq account and visible in that account's request history, with no notice or opt-in. Additionally, shipping a live `gsk_*` provider secret in import-reachable code distributes a third-party credential to every installer.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
